name: Pakistan
headline: Pakistan MONSOON WATCH DAY 2 CONFIRMATION — composite 20.3 / developing sustained. PDMA/ECHO confirmed active severe weather system across multiple provinces May 13; weather system continues through May 17; most active period May 13-15. NDMA active response. May 10-11 casualty baseline: 4 killed, 16 injured (KPK). No mass-casualty event confirmed in 24h window as of scan time. Monsoon watch expires today (day 2 of 2) — if no mass-casualty event documented by May 17 the watch closes. No score change; confirm at 20.3 / developing.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. PDMA/ECHO/NDMA active monitoring infrastructure operational. Pre-monsoon preparedness noted as positive sub-threshold AWR/A5 offset (carry-over from May 13).

EMP.rationale: Sustained. No mass-casualty event in window; baseline empathic-response capacity unchanged.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. NDMA active response per ECHO Daily Flash; PDMA active. Operational responsiveness sustained at baseline.

BND.rationale: Sustained. Indus Waters Treaty suspension carry-over (no new movement).

SYS.rationale: Sustained. NDMA system functioning; pre-monsoon preparedness positive sub-anchor.

evidence.summary: ECHO Daily Flash May 13 2026 (ReliefWeb): severe weather and flash floods confirmed across multiple provinces; PDMA active response; weather system continues through May 17.
NDMA situation reports portal — official Pakistan disaster authority real-time monitoring.

keyEvidence: May 13 ECHO Daily Flash: severe weather and flash floods confirmed active across multiple provinces.
Weather system continues through May 17; most active period May 13-15.
No mass-casualty event documented in 24h window as of scan time.
Monsoon watch day 2 of 2 — watch expires today.
rationale: Monsoon watch day 2 confirm at 20.3 / developing. No mass-casualty event documented in 24h window; PDMA/NDMA active response operational. If NDMA sitreps document significant casualties or displacement from the May 13-17 weather system by May 17, EMP dimension may face further downward pressure in a subsequent cycle. The pre-existing Indus Waters Treaty suspension and HRCP civil space documentation remain the primary pending assessment items.
watches: May 13-17 weather system mass-casualty outcome (close May 17)
boundaryWatchResolution: Composite 20.3 sits 0.7 below the developing-band threshold (21). Boundary-case-adjacent — but band placement currently noted as 'developing' in canonical data (likely reflects rounding tolerance at the band boundary). No new movement this cycle; watch continues.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
